summary: redis-type-os impersonates Redis Inc.'s redis-om package
details: Package name `redis-type-os` mimics the official Redis Inc. `redis-om` / `redis-om-node` package. package.json copies identity from the upstream project: `repository: github:redis/redis-om-node`, `homepage: https://github.com/redis/redis-om-node#readme`, and the shipped README/docs are verbatim redis-om documentation. The published tarball contains no code — `main` points at `dist/index.js` but no `dist/` directory exists, so `require('redis-type-os')` throws MODULE_NOT_FOUND. There are no install/preinstall/postinstall lifecycle scripts, so installing the package does not auto-execute any code. The package does declare a dependency on `nat-ulid` (an irregular substitute for the standard `ulid` package), which is pulled into the installer's dependency tree on install but does not match a known-malicious package. Without a concrete installer-harm payload (no exfil, no dropper, no silent-relay, no credential distribution), name-similarity alone is a subjective signal that warrants human review rather than an automatic block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
